description_long: This object consists of two weathered metal containers: one is a tall, cylindrical barrel with three prominent horizontal reinforcement bands and patches of rust, while the other is a shorter, wider bucket with a flat lid and a more matte, dark finish. Both containers show signs of wear, with chipped paint and surface corrosion, suggesting prolonged outdoor exposure. The barrel features a yellow spray-painted smiley face, while the bucket displays a red smiley face, both adding a touch of urban graffiti. The surfaces are rough and matte, with visible scratches and discoloration. The lids appear to be fixed or tightly fitted, and there are no visible handles on either container. These objects are typical of those found in industrial or urban outdoor environments, such as alleys, construction sites, or storage yards.
description: Two weathered metal containers, a tall barrel and a short bucket, both with rough, rusted surfaces and graffiti smiley faces. The barrel has reinforcement bands, while the bucket has a flat lid and no visible handles.
description_view_0: Front view showing the tall barrel and short bucket side by side, highlighting their height difference and weathered surfaces.
description_view_1: Angled view from the opposite side, emphasizing the flat lid of the bucket and the barrel's cylindrical shape.
description_view_2: View showing the graffiti: a yellow smiley face on the barrel and a red smiley face on the bucket.
description_view_3: Rear angled view, focusing on the rust patches on the barrel and the plain back of the bucket.
attribute_labeling_or_markings: yellow and red spray-painted smiley faces
